what_it_does: This AGENTS.md file documents the src/widgets/ subsystem: it lists the directory's key files, their responsibilities, and local conventions for composing widgets from a declarative registry while keeping third-party brand colors as literal data.
observations: registry.js exposes window.MS_REGISTRY for declarative widget specs meant to be registered once and reused everywhere
atoms.jsx contains tiny shared JSX building blocks used across widgets and workspaces
connectors.jsx exposes window.MS_CONNECTORS, an integration list for providers such as Drive
connectors carry each provider's own brand colour as literal hex data, not as a design token
Root CLAUDE.md and src/AGENTS.md also apply to this directory
The file explicitly instructs not to tokenize third-party brand colours
standouts: The rule that foreign brand colours are legitimate literal data and must not be turned into ConceptV tokens
The window.MS_REGISTRY pattern for declarative, reusable widget specs
The window.MS_CONNECTORS integration list carrying provider brand colours
conventions: Add widgets by registering a spec in registry.js rather than hand-building the same markup at call sites
Compose widget instances from the registry to avoid duplication
ConceptV chrome around widgets uses var(--*) / MS_TOKENS design tokens
Third-party brand colours in connector data stay literal hex values
concerns: The file asserts a declarative widget system but does not show the shape of a widget spec or how registration works
Global window objects (MS_REGISTRY, MS_CONNECTORS) are introduced without explaining initialization or lifecycle
No examples illustrate how atoms, connectors, source-intake, and registry interact
notes: This is the directory-level guide for the widgets package: it maps four files to their roles and draws a hard line between ConceptV tokens and literal third-party brand colours.
questions: What is the exact schema of a widget spec in window.MS_REGISTRY?
How does source-intake.jsx interact with connectors.jsx during upload or connection?
What does 'ConceptV chrome' refer to and which tokens are in MS_TOKENS?
Are MS_REGISTRY and MS_CONNECTORS initialized at application startup or lazily loaded?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (directory guide for the widgets subsystem) matches actual content: it lists files, responsibilities, and local conventions.
apparent_themes: Clear separation between owned design tokens and foreign brand data
Global registration surfaces for reusable widgets and integrations
Reuse and consistency across widgets and workspaces
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a data-driven widget ecosystem where widgets are registered once and composed everywhere, while preserving the integrity of third-party brand identities by keeping their colours as literal data rather than abstracting them into the design-token system.
novelty: The explicit, principled stance that third-party brand colours are data (not tokens) and should remain literal hex values is a distinct convention.
contribution: It defines the scope and responsibilities of the widgets subsystem, names the key registry and connector globals, and establishes the boundary between ConceptV design tokens and literal third-party brand colours — conventions a fused system would preserve.
summary_for_embedding: AGENTS.md for counterpart/project/src/widgets describes a declarative widget registry, shared atoms, connector integrations with literal brand colours, and a source-intake surface, along with token-versus-brand-colour conventions.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to orient agents to the widgets directory, promote composition from a shared registry, and prevent third-party brand colours from being incorrectly tokenized.
